title: Cephalanthera × mayeri
intro: Cephalanthera × mayeri, also known as the Mayer's Helleborine, is a rare and hybrid species of orchid native to Europe. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and combating infections. The bioactive constituents responsible for these properties include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, particularly Cephalanthera alkaloids and phenylethanoid glycosides. In traditional herbal medicine, Cephalanthera × mayeri is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Cephalanthera × mayeri is a hybrid orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. It is a natural hybrid between Cephalanthera caucasica and Cephalanthera rubra, two species of orchids. The classification of this hybrid orchid is as a nothospecies, denoted by the '×' symbol. Nothospecies are cross-bred hybrids that occur naturally in the wild, and their classification is not always clear-cut. The taxonomic classification of Cephalanthera × mayeri is still a subject of ongoing research and debate among botanists.
